data.title: Osisko Development announces senior management update
data.description:
• Osisko Development (NYSE:ODV) has announced the appointment of David Rouleau as Vice President, Project Development.
  • This appointment aligns with the Co.'s strategic focus on building an operations-driven team to advance its flagship 100%-owned Cariboo Gold Project, located in central British Columbia, Canada.
  • The Co. also announces that Éric Tremblay, COO, is leaving the Co. effective immediately to pursue other professional commitments in the mining sector.
  • In addition to his appointed role, Mr. Rouleau will provide senior leadership support during the transition period until a permanent COO is appointed, and he will take an active role in advancing operational readiness efforts for the Cariboo Gold Project.

data.title: Uniti commences offering of $589M secured fiber network revenue term notes
data.description:
• Specialized REIT Uniti Group (NASDAQ:UNIT) announced on Thursday the commencement of an offering of $589M worth of secured fiber network revenue term notes.
  • The offering will be conducted by the Uniti subsidiaries, Uniti Fiber ABS Issuer and Uniti Fiber TRS Issuer.
  • The notes are expected to have a repayment date in April 2030.
  • The notes will be secured by certain fiber network assets and related customer contracts in the State of Florida and the Gulf Coast region of Louisiana, Mississippi and Alabama.
  • The net proceeds are intended to be used to repay and terminate the company's existing ABS bridge facility and for general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Lumentum gets top pick tag in networking sector at Needham
data.description:
Needham named Lumentum (NASDAQ:LITE) as top pick for 2025 across its Networking and Cloud Communication sectors on surging AI investment.

The firm also added Lumentum to the Needham conviction list, replacing Harmonic (HLIT), while keeping a Buy rating and raising LITE's price target to $110.

Analysts led by Ryan Koontz said they expect Lumentum to benefit from continued strong growth in its Datacom business driven by surging AI infrastructure investments, and a sustained recovery in its telecom business, also largely driven by data center Wide Area Network, or WAN connectivity.

The analysts see expanding externally modulated lasers, or EML, production capacity as a major catalyst with the first mid-2025 tranche mostly priced in, but a second capacity tranche providing strong upside to 2026 estimates.

In addition, the analysts said that a likely higher U.S. tariffs on China open an opportunity to gain meaningful share in Datacom transceivers, or XCVRs.

data.title: CyberArk named top cybersecurity pick for 2025 by Needham
data.description:
CyberArk Software (NASDAQ:CYBR) was named the top stock for 2025 in the cybersecurity space by Needham Securities, amid continuing momentum.

"CYBR outperformed in CY24 (+54% vs. 27% IGV) but shares are well positioned for sustained momentum in CY25 driven by an accelerating threat environment, robust new logo growth, upside from Venafi cross-sell and synergies, a favorable consensus model setup, and continued strong execution against an advantageous competitive backdrop," Needham analyst Matt Dezort wrote in a note to clients.

Dezort also named CyberArk to Needham's Conviction List and upped his price target on the stock to $410 from $360.

Looking to 2025, Dezort said he thinks the initial annual recurring revenue guidance is likely to top the $1.39B estimate, as it includes the Venafi acquisition, but also implies a "modest" year-over-year decline in the company's core offerings, which is unlikely.

"CYBR has offered initial ARR guidance 1%-2%+ ahead of consensus each of the last few years, ultimately finishing the year 5%+ above original guidance," Deozrt added. "While we see more modest organic upside to estimates in CY25, the setup for beat and raise quarters throughout remains favorable, and we see at least mid-20% ARR growth as achievable."

data.title: AtkinsRéalis joint venture awarded $43M water program management services contract
data.description:
• AtkinsRéalis Group (TSX:ATRL:CA) on Thursday announced that Water Smart Alliance, a joint venture with Stantec, has been awarded a Water Program Management contract by the District of Columbia Water and Sewer Authority.
  • Under a $43-million contract, WSA will assist DC Water with refining and optimizing the water portion of their capital improvement plan.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Zentalis Pharmaceuticals and PureTech Health get Fast Track Designation from FDA for their cancer treatments
data.description:
• Zentalis Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:ZNTL) announced that the FDA has granted Fast Track Designation to azenosertib for treating platinum-resistant ovarian, fallopian tube, or primary peritoneal cancer with elevated Cyclin E1 protein levels.
  • PureTech Health (NASDAQ:PRTC) also said that the FDA has granted Fast Track designation to LYT-200 for the treatment of acute myeloid leukemia.
  • The FDA grants investigational medicines Fast Track Designation to facilitate the development and expedite the review of medicines that demonstrate the potential to treat serious conditions and fill an unmet medical need.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Blackstone makes $300M investment in AI data company - report
data.description:
Blackstone (NYSE:BX), the world's largest private equity firm, is investing $300M into DDN, valuing the Chatsworth, California-based AI data company at $5B, according to a media report.

DDN, which operates a platform to make AI more cost-effective, expects to use Blackstone's investment to expand its business-customer base, Alex Bouzari, one of its co-founders, told the Wall Street Journal.

The company's platform is being used in the Colossus supercomputer being built by Elon Musk's xAI in Memphis, Tennessee, among other projects. DDN could be a candidate for an initial public offering within a few years, the WSJ reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

The investment complements Blackstone's (NYSE:BX) strategy of investing in data centers and companies that support chipmakers. For example, in 2023, the firm entered a deal with Digital Realty (DLR) to develop $7B in data centers, and in September, it announced an agreement to acquire Asian data-center operator AirTrunk for $16B.

data.title: Oxy Capital acquires over 10% of Montero Mining and Exploration
data.description:
• Oxy Capital SGOIC on Thursday said it has acquired ownership and control of 29,800 common shares of Montero Mining and Exploration (TSXV:MON:CA) through purchases on the TSX Venture Exchange at a price of C$0.31 per common share for a purchase price of C$9,238, representing about 0.06% of the outstanding common shares.
  • Prior to the transaction, Oxy Capital had ownership and control over, 4.98M common shares, representing about 9.94% of the outstanding common shares.
  • Following the transaction, Oxy Capital now has ownership and control of a total of 5.01M common shares, representing about 10% of the outstanding common shares.
  • The common shares were acquired for investment purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: GXO signs partnership with Calliope in Italy
data.description:
• GXO Logistics (NYSE:GXO) has signed a long-term partnership with Calliope, a lifestyle brand of the Italian fashion franchise Teddy Group.
  • The contract includes warehouse management, e-fullfilment, returns management and value-added services such as labelling and inbound quality control.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Main Street Capital originates $123.4M of commitments in private loan portfolio in Q4
data.description:
• Business development company Main Street Capital (NYSE:MAIN) announced on Thursday the origination of new or increased commitments of $123.4M in its private loan portfolio in Q4.
  • The company funded investments across its private loan portfolio with a cost basis totaling $108.0M.
  • Source: Press Release
